mutations.change: Swap child zero and child one after every side decision while leaving all node rows unchanged.
mutations.reason: The independently frozen all-THINGS result document changes for broad map coverage.

mutations.change: Change the non-axis comparison from cross greater than zero to cross greater than or equal to zero.
mutations.reason: Origin and collinear equality cases are explicitly expected on side one.

mutations.change: Use only the generic cross expression for vertical and horizontal nodes, deleting their sign-sensitive branches.
mutations.reason: The hand table includes axis sign and equality combinations that generic cross does not preserve.

mutations.change: Invert the node dy sign tests in both vertical partition branches.
mutations.reason: Positive and negative vertical nodes have separately frozen left, right, and tie outcomes.

mutations.change: Invert the node dx sign tests in both horizontal partition branches.
mutations.reason: The pinned horizontal root has probes below, on, and above its partition.

mutations.change: Start traversal at node zero or minimum node id instead of the last node in the loaded NODES lump.
mutations.reason: The root id and every reviewed path signature begin at maximum node id 680.

mutations.change: Return the raw flagged child value as the subsector id without removing bit 0x8000.
mutations.reason: Returned subsector keys must be in the loaded zero-based 0 through 681 range.

mutations.change: Treat every child id as a subsector even when the high bit is clear, truncating traversal after one node.
mutations.reason: Reviewed paths have depths from five through twenty-five and cannot terminate at an ordinary node.

mutations.change: Resolve a subsector sector from its last seg rather than its first seg.
mutations.reason: The plan fixes first-seg sector ownership even where another seg could appear plausible.

mutations.change: Select the left sidedef for direction zero and the right sidedef for direction one.
mutations.reason: Facing direction is semantic and the known spawn sector anchors its correct interpretation.

mutations.change: Clamp input coordinates to map vertex bounds before traversing the BSP.
mutations.reason: BSP classification is defined for arbitrary numeric coordinates and must not depend on Spatial metadata bounds.

mutations.change: Round or truncate p_x and p_y before applying node predicates.
mutations.reason: Call-time numeric binds must reach the exact side predicates without fixture-driven normalization.

mutations.change: Replace the relational CONNECT BY traversal with a PL/SQL WHILE loop over nodes.
mutations.reason: The task explicitly requires relational CONNECT BY traversal and forbids a procedural substitute.

mutations.change: Special-case known THINGS coordinates or consult a precomputed expected sector lookup instead of traversing nodes.
mutations.reason: Fixture-specific lookup is reward hacking and cannot generalize to boundary or held-back transformed probes.

mutations.change: Aggregate traversed nodes without an explicit root-to-leaf order when constructing the path signature.
mutations.reason: Stable path output must not depend on row order or optimizer choices.
